name: Oportun
description_short: Oportun Houston, Texas — Oportun offers in-person personal loans — secured and unsecured — with store locations nationwide, Spanish-language support, an...
description_long:
Oportun is a consumer lender with a physical branch network spanning all 50 US states and Washington D.C. The company operates storefront locations where borrowers can apply in person, distinguishing it from purely digital lenders. Oportun also supports a Spanish-speaking customer base and provides a mobile app with a referral incentive program.

Oportun offers two primary loan types visible on its site: standard personal loans and secured personal loans backed by a vehicle title. In-person applicants need a valid government-issued photo ID (driver's license, passport, or consular ID), proof of income and address via pay stubs, bank statements, or utility bills, and — for secured loans — the car title in their name, registration, and photos of the vehicle. Applications are taken at branch locations and supported by phone and live chat.

One of Oportun's most distinctive features is its broad cash payment network. Borrowers can make payments at grocery stores, corner stores, pharmacies, and hundreds of other retail locations through five payment partners: Dolex, Barri, CheckFreePay, MoneyGram (receive code 7991oportun), and PayNearMe. All payment spots accept cash, and borrowers simply bring their loan number or barcode. This network is particularly valuable for customers who are unbanked or underbanked and rely on cash for bill payments. PayNearMe charges a fee of $1.75 for cash or $1.49 for debit transactions.

Oportun fills a genuine gap for borrowers who prefer or require in-person service, speak primarily Spanish, or pay bills in cash outside the traditional banking system. The secured loan option expands access but carries real collateral risk — defaulting could mean losing the vehicle. The locations page reviewed here does not publish APR ranges or loan amounts, so borrowers will need to visit a branch or call (866) 488-6090 to understand the full cost of borrowing before committing.

As a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
address: 10915 East Fwy Suite A, Houston, TX 77029

company_info.headquarters: 10915 East Fwy Suite A, Houston, TX 77029

pros: Physical store locations across all 50 states and DC — borrowers can apply in person with a loan officer
Cash payment accepted at hundreds of retail locations including grocery stores, pharmacies, and corner stores
Five cash payment network partners: Dolex, Barri, CheckFreePay, MoneyGram, and PayNearMe
Secured loan option available using car title as collateral, potentially enabling larger loan access
Spanish-language support available (Español option on site and in stores)
Mobile app with $50 referral bonus program for existing customers
Phone support at (866) 488-6090 and live chat for customer service
cons: APR ranges, loan amounts, and fees are not published on the locations page — must visit in person or call to compare costs
Secured loan puts the borrower's vehicle at risk of repossession if they default
PayNearMe cash payments carry a $1.75 fee, adding cost for customers who rely on cash
In-person application requires gathering multiple documents (ID, income proof, address proof) before visiting a branch
Primarily in-store experience is less convenient than fully digital lenders for borrowers with reliable internet access
best_for: Borrowers who prefer face-to-face application over online-only processes
Spanish-speaking consumers seeking a lender with native language support
Unbanked or underbanked borrowers who pay bills in cash at retail locations
Consumers with a vehicle willing to use it as collateral for a secured personal loan
diagnosis: Oportun is best suited for borrowers who prefer in-person service, are Spanish-speaking, or rely on cash-based bill payment at retail locations. The secured loan option can open doors for those who need larger amounts but are willing to pledge their vehicle. Borrowers who can manage a fully digital process should comparison-shop on APR before choosing Oportun, as rates are not disclosed upfront.
services: In-store loan applications at physical branches
Cash bill payment at grocery stores, pharmacies, and corner stores
PayNearMe mobile cash and debit payment option
Mobile app with referral bonus program
Phone and live chat customer support
